---
title: "Myeloma CTA: MINDENOM Family Walkthrough"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

<!-- TODO (Slice L artifact pipeline):
  This article depends on inst/extdata/articles/myeloma_fits.rds, which has
  not yet been generated.

  To unblock:
    1. Create data-raw/build_article_artifacts.R that fits myeloma at
       MINDENOM=1, 30, 56 with the canonical settings (see CLAUDE.md §Myeloma)
       and saves to inst/extdata/articles/myeloma_fits.rds.
    2. Expand the sections below using actual fit objects and
       cta_confusion_table() / cta_endpoint_summary() calls on those objects.
    3. Confirm anchor values: MINDENOM=1 WESS=27.69%, MINDENOM=30 WESS=16.51%,
       MINDENOM=56 no tree.

  See: docs/myeloma-cta-translation.md for all canonical computed values.
  See: CLAUDE.md §Myeloma for canonical fit settings and confusion anchors.
-->

**Status:** Draft — precomputed artifacts not yet generated.
This article will walk through the myeloma CTA MINDENOM family (MINDENOM = 1,
30, 56) as a complete canon example once `inst/extdata/articles/myeloma_fits.rds`
is available.

**Canonical anchor values (from CTA.exe, verified in test suite):**

| MINDENOM | Tree | Classified n | OVERALL ESS | WEIGHTED ESS |
|----------|------|-------------|-------------|--------------|
| 1 | V14→V15 (pruned: V17→V15) | 255 (pruned: 186) | — | 27.69% (pruned: 25.43%) |
| 30 | V17 stump | 186 | 15.99% | 16.51% |
| 56 | no tree | — | — | — |

**Source material:**

- `tests/testthat/fixtures/myeloma/` — myeloma fixture data and CTA.exe outputs
- `docs/myeloma-cta-translation.md` — full walkthrough with all computed values
- `docs/CTA_CANON.md` — MINDENOM anchor values and confusion matrices

---

## The myeloma dataset

*(To be expanded with artifact pipeline.)*

The myeloma dataset has n = 255 patients (after excluding V2 = 0 zero-weight
rows), a binary class variable (0 vs. 1), ten ordered attributes (V4, V9,
V11–V12, V14–V19), case weights in V2, and missing value code −9. V17 has
69 missing values, so any tree using V17 as the root classifies only 186
observations.

## MINDENOM = 1: V14 → V15 tree

*(To be expanded.)*

Root split: V14 ≤ 0.5 routes 200 observations left, 55 right. The left
branch is further split by V15 ≤ 0.5. The pruned tree uses V17 as root
instead (WESS 25.43%), classifying only 186 observations.

## MINDENOM = 30: V17 stump

*(To be expanded.)*

V17 right child has n = 55 < 56 (MINDENOM=56) but n = 55 ≥ 30 (MINDENOM=30).
Result: V17 stump, confusion [[101, 34], [30, 21]], OVERALL ESS = 15.99%,
WEIGHTED ESS = 16.51%.

## MINDENOM = 56: no tree

*(To be expanded.)*

All candidate root splits produce at least one child with n < 56. No admissible
split exists; the result is a leaf node only.

## WESS vs. OVERALL ESS: terminology note

When `WEIGHT` is active, the primary scoring metric is **WEIGHTED ESS** (WESS).
OVERALL ESS uses raw counts and is reported alongside WESS for reference. Do
not call WESS "ESS" or call OVERALL ESS "WESS" — the distinction matters when
comparing across MINDENOM levels.
